summary: Postinstall exfiltrates full environment and host metadata to Burp Collaborator OAST endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js enumerates every process.env variable (including credentials such as AWS_*, NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN and other CI tokens) and collects host fingerprint data — hostname, username, homedir, cwd, argv, and platform — then POSTs the JSON payload to https://rytxau88zxh61dw6qz4yn19naeg54vsk.oastify.com/. The destination is a Burp Suite Collaborator (oastify.com) out-of-band interaction host, used here as an attacker-controlled exfiltration sink. The package name `@0xlr/vercel-analytics` impersonates Vercel's `@vercel/analytics`, and the 999.0.0 version plus the self-described `Placeholder reservation` text are the canonical shape of a weaponized dependency-confusion squat designed to override an internal package of the same unscoped name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
